---
title: "gaussian_curvature"
generated: true
description: "Per-vertex Gaussian curvature $K$ via the angle-defect formula."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.geometry.surface`](../nitrix-geometry-surface.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:gaussian_curvature -->
```python
gaussian_curvature(mesh: Mesh, *, area_scheme: Literal['voronoi', 'barycentric'] = 'voronoi') -> Float[Array, 'n_vertices']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:gaussian_curvature -->
Per-vertex Gaussian curvature $K$ via the angle-defect formula.

$K(v_i) = (2 \pi - \sum_j \theta_{ij}) / A_i$ where
$\theta_{ij}$ are the interior triangle angles incident at
$v_i$ and $A_i$ the vertex area.  The numerator (the angle
defect) is the *integrated* Gaussian curvature, so
$\sum_i K_i A_i = 2 \pi \chi$ exactly (discrete Gauss-Bonnet) --
$4 \pi$ for a genus-0 surface.

Assumes a **closed** mesh (the $2 \pi$ term); for a surface with
boundary the boundary vertices would need the $\pi$ defect instead.
The geometry suite's targets (icosphere, genus-0 cortical surfaces) are
closed.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:gaussian_curvature -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `mesh` | `Mesh` | — | — | Triangle mesh (closed). |
| `area_scheme` | `Literal['voronoi', 'barycentric']` | — | `'voronoi'` | Vertex-area scheme for $A_i$ (`'voronoi'` default). |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `n_vertices`. Per-vertex Gaussian curvature, shape `(n_vertices,)`. Pure JAX; differentiable.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:gaussian_curvature -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n_vertices` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
